# Proof Certainty Engine

Skeptical buyers do not need to believe the ad.

They need to believe the proof path after the ad.

## Proof asset hierarchy

| Proof asset | Strength | Why |
|---|---:|---|
| Live interactive demo | Very high | Buyer can inspect. |
| Product sandbox | Very high | Buyer verifies by doing. |
| Public docs | High | Reduces implementation uncertainty. |
| Transparent pricing | High | Reduces hidden-risk fear. |
| Customer case study with numbers | High | Shows reality, if credible. |
| Reviews from third parties | Medium-high | Less controlled by seller. |
| Founder technical walkthrough | Medium-high | Builds mechanism trust. |
| Screenshot | Medium | Concrete but limited. |
| Generic testimonial | Low-medium | Often vague. |
| Ad claim alone | Low | Buyer has no reason to believe it. |

## Proof path formula

```text
Claim → Mechanism → Demonstration → Independent signal → Low-risk next step
```

Example:

```text
Claim: Clean support tickets faster.
Mechanism: AI extracts customer, urgency, reproduction steps, and system logs.
Demonstration: 90-second real ticket transformation.
Independent signal: Public customer review or case snippet.
Next step: Paste one ticket and see the generated Linear issue.
```

## Agent rule

Every campaign needs a proof path file.

If the campaign only has ads and no proof path, it is not launch-ready.
